results.title: Liberation
results.description: Liberation can imply a temporary release of the mind, i.e. liberated from certain unwholesome mental qualities or complete liberation from all unwholesome qualities of the mind, i.e. Nibbāna.

results.title: Mahāvedalla sutta - The Greater Series Of Questions And Answers
results.description: A series of questions and answers between Venerable Sāriputta and Venerable Mahākoṭṭhita that clarify subtle yet important aspects of the teachings. Topics covered include wisdom, consciousness, felt
results.contentSnippet: Rāgo kho, āvuso, pamāṇakaraṇo, doso pamāṇakaraṇo, moho pamāṇakaraṇo. Te khīṇāsavassa bhikkhuno pahīnā ucchinnamūlā tālāvatthukatā anabhāvaṅkatā āyatiṁ anuppādadhammā. Yāvatā kho, āvuso, appamāṇā cetov

results.title: Bhikkhu sutta - Bhikkhu
results.description: The Buddha explains how even for an arahant, acquisitions, respect, and popularity are an obstacle to the attainment of pleasant abidings in this very life.
results.contentSnippet: “Yā hissa sā, ānanda, <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">akuppā</mark> cetovimutti nāhaṁ tassā lābhasakkārasilokaṁ antarāyāya vadāmi. Ye ca khvassa,

results.title: Indriya sutta - Mental Faculties
results.description: The Buddha describes the three mental faculties - 1) the faculty that senses ‘I will know the unknown,’ 2) the faculty of awakening, and 3) the faculty of one who is awakened.
results.contentSnippet:
Tato aññāvimuttassa,
ñāṇaṁ ve hoti tādino;
<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">Akuppā</mark> me vimuttīti,
bhavasaṁyojanakkhayā.

results.title: Mahāsāropama sutta - The Greater Discourse on the Simile of the Heartwood
results.description: After Devadatta’s departure, the Buddha taught that the spiritual life is not pursued for acquisitions, respect, popularity, ethical conduct, collectedness, or knowledge and vision. Through the simile
results.contentSnippet: Iti kho, bhikkhave, nayidaṁ brahmacariyaṁ lābhasakkārasilokānisaṁsaṁ, na sīlasampadānisaṁsaṁ, na samādhisampadānisaṁsaṁ, na ñāṇadassanānisaṁsaṁ. Yā ca kho ayaṁ, bhikkhave, <mark class="bg-yellow-100 d

results.title: Upakkilesa sutta - Impurity
results.description: When the Buddha fails to achieve reconciliation among quarrelsome bhikkhus at Kosambi, he withdraws into solitude and later encounters an inspiring community of monks devoted to liberation. He teaches
results.contentSnippet: Yato kho me, anuruddhā, savitakkopi savicāro samādhi bhāvito ahosi, avitakkopi vicāramatto samādhi bhāvito ahosi, avitakkopi avicāro samādhi bhāvito ahosi, sappītikopi samādhi bhāvito ahosi, nippītiko

results.title: Cūḷasāropama sutta - The Shorter Discourse on the Simile of the Heartwood
results.description: When questioned about rival teachers, the Buddha sets the debate aside to explain the true purpose of the spiritual life through the simile of the heartwood. He cautions that settling for fame, ethica
results.contentSnippet: Iti kho, brāhmaṇa, nayidaṁ brahmacariyaṁ lābhasakkārasilokānisaṁsaṁ, na sīlasampadānisaṁsaṁ, na samādhisampadānisaṁsaṁ, na ñāṇadassanānisaṁsaṁ. Yā ca kho ayaṁ, brāhmaṇa, <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dar

results.title: Pāsarāsi sutta - The Noble Quest
results.description: The Buddha shares his own journey of seeking the path to awakening, from leaving the household life, to studying under two meditation teachers, to attaining full awakening and an account of teaching t
results.contentSnippet: Ñāṇañca pana me dassanaṁ udapādi: ‘<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">akuppā</mark> me vimutti, ayamantimā jāti, natthi dāni punabbhavo’ti.

results.title: Dhammacakkappavattana sutta - Setting in Motion of the Wheel of Dhamma
results.description: The Buddha’s first discourse to the group of five bhikkhus at the Deer Park in Isipatana, near Varanasi. The discourse explains the Four Noble Truths and the Noble Eightfold Path in brief. It ends wit
results.contentSnippet: Ñāṇañca pana me dassanaṁ udapādi: ‘<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">akuppā</mark> me vimutti, ayamantimā jāti, natthi dāni punabbhavo’”ti.

results.title: Dhātuvibhaṅga sutta - Exposition on the Elements
results.description: In a chance meeting, the Buddha, unrecognized by the bhikkhu Pukkusāti, teaches him to deconstruct experience into six elements, six fields of contact, eighteen mental explorations, and four foundatio
results.contentSnippet: Tassa sā vimutti sacce ṭhitā <mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">akuppā</mark> hoti. Tañhi, bhikkhu, musā yaṁ mosadhammaṁ, taṁ saccaṁ yaṁ amosadhammaṁ

results.title: Pubbevasambodha sutta - Before Full Awakening
results.description: Before his awakening, the Bodhisatta reflected on the gratification in the world, the drawback in the world, and the escape from it.
results.contentSnippet: Ñāṇañca pana me dassanaṁ udapādi: ‘<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">akuppā</mark> me vimutti, ayamantimā jāti, natthi dāni punabbhavo’”ti.

results.title: Paṭhama assāda sutta - Gratification (First)
results.description: The Buddha describes his quest for gratification in the world, drawback in the world, and the escape from it. He subsequently experientially realized gratification, drawback, and escape as they truly
results.contentSnippet: Ñāṇañca pana me dassanaṁ udapādi: ‘<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">akuppā</mark> me vimutti, ayamantimā jāti, natthi dāni punabbhavo’”ti.

results.title: Pubbesambodha sutta - Before Awakening
results.description: Before his awakening, the Buddha reflected on the gratification, drawback, and escape in regard to the four elements of earth, water, fire, and air.
results.contentSnippet: Ñāṇañca pana me dassanaṁ udapādi: ‘<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">akuppā</mark> me vimutti, ayamantimā jāti, natthi dāni punabbhavo’”ti.

results.title: Assāda sutta - Gratification (First)
results.description: Only after fully understanding the gratification, drawback, and escape in the case of form, felt experience, perception, intentional constructs, and consciousness, the Buddha declared that he had atta
results.contentSnippet: Ñāṇañca pana me dassanaṁ udapādi: ‘<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">akuppā</mark> me vimutti; ayamantimā jāti; natthi dāni punabbhavo’”ti.

results.title: Acariṁ sutta - Investigation
results.description: The Buddha explains how he investigated the four elements of earth, water, fire and air to understand the full extent of gratification, drawback, and escape in regard to each of them.
results.contentSnippet: Ñāṇañca pana me dassanaṁ udapādi: ‘<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">akuppā</mark> me vimutti, ayamantimā jāti, natthi dāni punabbhavo’”ti.

results.title: Mahāhatthipadopama sutta - The Greater Discourse on the Simile of the Elephant’s Footprint
results.description: Venerable Sāriputta explains how all wholesome teachings are encompassed by the Four Noble Truths. He then explains the four great elements of earth, water, fire and wind.
results.contentSnippet: Hoti kho so, āvuso, samayo yaṁ bāhirā āpodhātu p<mark class="bg-yellow-100 dark:bg-yellow-900 rounded" style="padding-inline:0.05rem">akuppa</mark>ti. Antarahitā tasmiṁ samaye bāhirā pathavīdhātu hoti

results.title: Udakūpamā sutta - Comparable to Water
results.description: The Buddha describes seven kinds of persons and likens them to those in water — from those submerged in unwholesomeness to those who cross over fully. They represent the stages from spiritual stagnati

results.title: Paṭiccasamuppāda sutta - Dependent Co-Arising
results.description: The Buddha explains the twelve links of dependent co-arising, and how there is an arising and ending of the whole mass of suffering.

results.title: Āsāduppajaha vagga - The Chapter Beginning With The Difficult to Abandon
results.description: The Buddha teaches on two hopes that are difficult to abandon, two kinds of people who are rare in the world, two kinds of people who are difficult to satisfy, two causes for the arising of passion, a

results.title: Āghātavatthu sutta - Grounds for Resentment
results.description: The Buddha shares the ten grounds for resentment that lead to anger and agitation in the mind.
